name: use-key
title: useKey
description: Bind keyboard shortcuts to handlers with scoped, document-level, or container-scoped listening. Supports single key, array of keys, or key map overloads.

content.text: Options for registering one or more hotkeys with `useKey`.
* Explicit keyboard scope to register under. Pass null to skip registration.
* Defaults to the nearest active provider scope for this hook's declaration order.
* Registers a single hotkey with the nearest `KeyboardProvider`.
* Without a provider this hook is a no-op.
* Registers several hotkeys with one handler through the nearest
Registers a map of hotkeys to handlers through the nearest `KeyboardProvider`.

biome-ignore lint/correctness/useExhaustiveDependencies: deps are intentionally the stable primitive options plus the derived `registrationVersion` string; depending on the per-render `handlerMap`/`registrationKeys`/`handlerOptions` objects would re-register on every render. Latest handlers are read via the stable `dispatch` effect event.

docs.description: Bind keyboard shortcuts to handlers with scoped, document-level, or container-scoped listening. Supports single key, array of keys, or key map overloads.

parameters.name: hotkey
parameters.description: A single key combo, an array of key combos, or a map of key combos to handlers.

parameters.name: handler
parameters.description: Callback invoked when the hotkey fires. Not used with the key-map overload.

parameters.name: options.scope
parameters.description: Explicit keyboard scope to register the binding under. Pass null to skip registration. Defaults to the nearest active KeyboardProvider scope for this hook's declaration order.

parameters.name: options.allowInInput
parameters.description: Allow the hotkey to fire when an input, textarea, or contenteditable element is focused.

parameters.name: options.containerRef
parameters.description: DOM boundary used by focusWithinOnly to restrict when the handler fires.

parameters.name: options.focusWithinOnly
parameters.description: Only fire the handler when focus is within the container element.

parameters.name: options.preventDefault
parameters.description: Call preventDefault() on the keyboard event when the hotkey matches.

returns.description: This hook does not return a value.

notes.title: Three overloads
notes.content: useKey supports three call signatures: single key + handler, array of keys + handler, and a Record<string, KeyHandler> map (no separate handler argument).

notes.title: Provider-dependent
notes.content: useKey registers handlers when a KeyboardProvider is present. Without a provider it is a no-op.

notes.title: Scope-aware
notes.content: Handlers respect the scope stack managed by useScope. Deeper scopes take priority over shallower ones.

notes.title: Declining a match
notes.content: Return exactly false from a handler to let the next lower-priority matching handler in the active scope run. Other return values are ignored. If preventDefault is enabled for that handler, the event is still prevented before the handler returns.

content.text: ArrowUp increments, ArrowDown decrements, Escape resets.
The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.
Ctrl+B bold, Ctrl+I italic, Ctrl+U underline, Esc reset.
focusWithinOnly + containerRef: the shortcut only fires while focus is
inside this card, so two editors on one page do not collide.
Click into the textarea, then press Ctrl+S. Saves:
Each card owns its own Ctrl+S; the binding is inert unless its card has focus.
